content.text: I want a clean visual map of this conversation with clear branches and readable summaries.
I’ll collapse the session into branch nodes, summarize the intent of each path, and render it as a polished HTML tree.
Give me a few directions: one focused on top-down readability, one on interaction, and one on visual polish.
I see three promising directions: a top-down tree, an interactive zoomable map, and a polished card-based layout with richer metadata badges.
Maybe lean hard into interaction: add zoom, search, and a detail panel.
That path becomes more like a mini app: zoom controls, panning, search, and per-node drill-down details.

summary: Interactive exploration direction with zoom, search, and click-to-inspect details.

content.text: What about maximum visual polish while keeping the same structure?
That means better typography, gentler depth, cleaner badges, more atmospheric background treatment, and stronger hover behavior.
Keep the top-down layout. I want something that feels beautiful but dead simple to read.
Great. I’ll keep the top-down tree, center parents above children, and make the cards feel calm and editorial instead of dashboard-y.

summary: Top-down card tree chosen for clarity and visual quality.

content.text: Nice. Now tune the colors, highlight the active path, and make the badges feel useful instead of noisy.
Done. I’ll use a dark atmospheric canvas, subtle grid texture, blue active-path treatment, green active-leaf glow, and compact metadata badges for turns, tools, words, and duration.
